title: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: 5.5% reflects realistic, single-digit expansion for a mature titan. The company's pipeline and strategic acquisitions in cardiovascular tech (like Shockwave Medical) provide steady upside, perfectly offsetting the inevitable patent cliffs of blockbuster drugs like Stelara. The scale of JNJ prevents explosive percentage growth.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: <div class="assumption-grid" data-astro-cid-w32zjhjs> <div class="assumption-card" data-astro-cid-w32zjhjs> <div class="card-title" data-astro-cid-w32zjhjs>FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 2.5% is an alignment with global healthcare spending and long-term inflation. JNJ is fundamentally tied to the aging demographics of the developed world. It will continue to grow at or slightly above global GDP in perpetuity.

thesisTitle: The AI Thesis: Safe Haven Reliability
thesisParagraphs: Johnson & Johnson represents the ultimate defensive stock. As one of only two U.S. corporations retaining an elite AAA credit rating, it is often viewed as being as financially secure as the U.S. government. Following the spin-off of its consumer health division (Kenvue), JNJ is now a highly focused, higher-margin entity concentrating exclusively on Innovative Medicine and MedTech.

This valuation model doesn't assume hyper-growth; rather, it prices in the immense value of certainty. In an increasingly volatile macro environment, JNJ's ability to consistently generate nearly $20B in Free Cash Flow annually commands a premium multiple by way of a heavily discounted WACC.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).
